verdict.answer_first: A gets to the actionable sequence sooner with “The plan” followed by “Model your plans in Stripe first”; B spends a “mental model” section before implementation.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits cleanly to defaults with “assuming recurring subscriptions, hosted Stripe Checkout”; B also recommends Checkout but opens more framing with “where it changes if your situation differs.”
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it adds concrete implementation traps and decisions: “raw body, before JSON parsing,” “dedupe on event ID,” and “client sends a plan identifier, the server maps it.”
